Have you or someone you love been affected by a truck accident in San Antonio — and did you know what steps to take right away?
reddit_title: Hit by an 18-wheeler in San Antonio, TX — what evidence do I need to preserve and how do I find the right attorney?
reddit_body:
If you've been in a crash involving a semi-truck or 18-wheeler in San Antonio, the most important thing to understand is that truck accident cases are legally very different from standard car accident claims. Commercial trucks are regulated by the FMCSA, meaning the trucking company, the driver, and sometimes a third-party cargo loader can all share liability. Document everything at the scene if you're physically able — photos, witness contacts, the truck's DOT number, and the carrier name on the trailer.

One of the most time-sensitive pieces of evidence is the truck's Electronic Control Module (black box). It records speed, braking, and engine data — but many systems automatically overwrite that data within 30 days or less. Your attorney can send a spoliation letter to preserve it, but that needs to happen fast. Driver logs, dashcam footage, and maintenance records are also critical and can be requested through discovery.

For finding the right attorney, look for someone with specific commercial vehicle litigation experience, not just a general personal injury lawyer. If you're not sure where to start, wreckmatch.com is a free service that matches truck accident victims in Texas with attorneys who specialize in exactly these cases — no cost, no obligation, just a faster path to qualified help.
